canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, T-REx is consistently characterized as a dataset used for evaluation. The prevailing description is that of a "slot filling dataset," a framing found in multiple papers. Its primary application is to evaluate a model's ability to perform "factual relation extraction-style prediction" from text or knowledge. One paper also uses it to measure the broader behavior of "Information extraction". While most definitions focus on its function, one provides more detail on its construction, describing it as a "large-scale slot-filling dataset derived from Wikipedia abstracts and aligned with Wikidata triples". Another source situates it within a larger collection of benchmarks, noting its inclusion in "the KILT subset and evaluation tasks".
papers: Think-on-Graph: Deep and Responsible Reasoning of Large Language Model on Knowledge Graph
A Theory for Token-Level Harmonization in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
Long-Context LLMs Meet RAG: Overcoming Challenges for Long Inputs in RAG
RAG-DDR: Optimizing Retrieval-Augmented Generation Using Differentiable Data Rewards
Reasoning of Large Language Models over Knowledge Graphs with Super-Relations
Scalable Influence and Fact Tracing for Large Language Model Pretraining

definitions.text: A slot filling dataset used to evaluate factual relation extraction-style prediction from text or knowledge.

relationships.paper: PBI-Attack: Prior-Guided Bimodal Interactive Black-Box Jailbreak Attack for Toxicity Maximization
